﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: AWS CodePipeline — buildspec.yml for CodeBuild
# WHEN TO USE: When using the codepipeline.yml CloudFormation template
# PREREQUISITES: Dockerfile in the project root
# SECRETS NEEDED: None (injected via CodeBuild environment)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: cd/targets/aws-codepipeline/codepipeline.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================
#
# Place this file in your project root if you prefer an external
# buildspec instead of the inline one in codepipeline.yml.
# Then update the CodeBuild Source in the CloudFormation template:
#   Source:
#     Type: CODEPIPELINE
#     BuildSpec: buildspec.yml
#

# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
version: 0.2

# Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
env:
  # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  variables:
    # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    DOCKER_BUILDKIT: "1"          # Enable BuildKit for faster builds

# Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
phases:
  # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  install:
    # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    runtime-versions:
      # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      docker: 20                   # <-- CHANGE THIS: match your Docker version needs

  # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  pre_build:
    # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    commands:
      # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - echo "--- Pre-build phase ---"
      # Note 12: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - echo "Logging in to Amazon ECR..."
      # Note 13: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - aws ecr get-login-password --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com
      # Note 14: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - IMAGE_URI=$AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com/$ECR_REPOSITORY
      # Note 15: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - IMAGE_TAG=$CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION
      # Note 16: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - echo "Image URI = $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG"

      # Run unit tests before building the image
      # Uncomment and adapt for your stack:
      # - dotnet test                          # .NET
      # - npm ci && npm test                   # Node.js
      # - pip install -r requirements.txt && pytest  # Python
      # - ./gradlew test                       # Java/Gradle

  # Note 17: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  build:
    # Note 18: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    commands:
      # Note 19: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - echo "--- Build phase ---"
      # Note 20: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - echo "Building Docker image..."
      # Note 21: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - docker build -t $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG -f Dockerfile .   # <-- CHANGE THIS: Dockerfile path
      - docker tag $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG $IMAGE_URI:latest

  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo "--- Post-build phase ---"
      - echo "Pushing Docker image to ECR..."
      - docker push $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG
      - docker push $IMAGE_URI:latest
      - echo "Generating imagedefinitions.json for ECS deploy..."
      - printf '[{"name":"%s","imageUri":"%s"}]' "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG" > imagedefinitions.json
      - cat imagedefinitions.json

artifacts:
  files:
    - imagedefinitions.json
    # - appspec.yml                  # Uncomment if using CodeDeploy (blue/green)
    # - taskdef.json                 # Uncomment if using CodeDeploy (blue/green)

reports:
  # Uncomment to publish test results to CodeBuild Reports
  # test-results:
  #   files:
  #     - "**/*.xml"                 # JUnit, NUnit, pytest XML reports
  #   file-format: JUNITXML
  #   base-directory: test-results   # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to test output

cache:
  paths:
    - "/root/.docker/**/*"           # Docker layer cache
    # - "node_modules/**/*"          # Uncomment for Node.js
    # - "/root/.cache/pip/**/*"      # Uncomment for Python
    # - "/root/.nuget/**/*"          # Uncomment for .NET

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: AWS CodePipeline — Build, Test, Deploy (CloudFormation)
# WHEN TO USE: Teams using AWS-native CI/CD instead of GitHub/GitLab
# PREREQUISITES: CodeStar Connection to repo, ECR repository, ECS/EKS target
# SECRETS NEEDED: None (uses IAM roles)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: cd/targets/aws-ecs/, cd/targets/aws-eks/, terraform/aws-ecs/
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================
#
# This CloudFormation template creates a full CodePipeline with:
#   Source (GitHub/CodeCommit) → Build (CodeBuild) → Deploy (ECS or EKS)
#
# Deploy this template:
#   aws cloudformation deploy \
#     --template-file codepipeline.yml \
#     --stack-name my-app-pipeline \
#     --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
#     --parameter-overrides \
#         ProjectName=my-app \
#         RepositoryId=my-org/my-repo \
#         BranchName=main \
#         EcsClusterName=my-cluster \
#         EcsServiceName=my-service
#

# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "CI/CD Pipeline — CodePipeline + CodeBuild → ECS Fargate"

# ============================================================
# PARAMETERS
# ============================================================
Parameters:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  ProjectName:
    Type: String
    Description: "Project name — used for naming all resources"
    # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    Default: my-app                # <-- CHANGE THIS

  RepositoryId:
    Type: String
    # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    Description: "GitHub repository (org/repo format) or CodeCommit repo name"
    Default: my-org/my-repo        # <-- CHANGE THIS

  BranchName:
    # Note 5: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
    Type: String
    Description: "Branch to trigger the pipeline"
    Default: main

  # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  ConnectionArn:
    Type: String
    Description: "ARN of the CodeStar Connection to GitHub (create in Console → Settings → Connections)"
    # <-- CHANGE THIS: create connection first, then paste ARN

  # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  EcrRepositoryName:
    Type: String
    Description: "ECR repository name for built images"
    # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    Default: my-app                # <-- CHANGE THIS

  EcsClusterName:
    Type: String
    # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    Description: "ECS cluster to deploy to"
    Default: my-cluster            # <-- CHANGE THIS

  EcsServiceName:
    # Note 10: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
    Type: String
    Description: "ECS service to update"
    Default: my-service            # <-- CHANGE THIS

  # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  ContainerName:
    Type: String
    Description: "Container name in the ECS task definition"
    # Note 12: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    Default: my-app                # <-- CHANGE THIS

  BuildComputeType:
    Type: String
    # Note 13: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    Description: "CodeBuild compute type"
    Default: BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL
    AllowedValues:
      # Note 14: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL
      - BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM
      - BUILD_GENERAL1_LARGE

# Note 15: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
Resources:
  # ============================================================
  # S3 Bucket — stores pipeline artifacts between stages
  # ============================================================
  ArtifactBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    # Note 16: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${ProjectName}-pipeline-artifacts-${AWS::AccountId}"
      VersioningConfiguration:
        # Note 17: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        Status: Enabled
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          # Note 18: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        # Note 19: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        Rules:
          - Id: CleanupOldArtifacts
            Status: Enabled
            # Note 20: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            ExpirationInDays: 30
      Tags:
        - Key: Project
          # Note 21: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          Value: !Ref ProjectName
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: cloudformation

  # ============================================================
  # IAM Role — CodeBuild
  # ============================================================
  # Note 22: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  CodeBuildRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      # Note 23: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      RoleName: !Sub "${ProjectName}-codebuild-role"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        # Note 24: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              # Note 25: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              Service: codebuild.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        # Note 26: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        - PolicyName: CodeBuildPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            # Note 27: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            Statement:
              # CloudWatch Logs
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  # Note 28: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                # Note 29: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/aws/codebuild/${ProjectName}-build*"
              # S3 artifacts
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  # Note 30: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetBucketAcl
                  # Note 31: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - s3:GetBucketLocation
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt ArtifactBucket.Arn
                  # Note 32: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - !Sub "${ArtifactBucket.Arn}/*"
              # ECR — push images
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  # Note 33: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - ecr:GetAuthorizationToken
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                # Note 34: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Action:
                  - ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability
                  - ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer
                  # Note 35: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - ecr:BatchGetImage
                  - ecr:PutImage
                  - ecr:InitiateLayerUpload
                  # Note 36: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - ecr:UploadLayerPart
                  - ecr:CompleteLayerUpload
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:ecr:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:repository/${EcrRepositoryName}"

  # ============================================================
  # IAM Role — CodePipeline
  # ============================================================
  # Note 37: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  CodePipelineRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      # Note 38: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      RoleName: !Sub "${ProjectName}-codepipeline-role"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        # Note 39: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              # Note 40: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              Service: codepipeline.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        # Note 41: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        - PolicyName: CodePipelinePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            # Note 42: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            Statement:
              # S3 artifacts
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  # Note 43: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetBucketVersioning
                # Note 44: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt ArtifactBucket.Arn
                  - !Sub "${ArtifactBucket.Arn}/*"
              # CodeBuild
              # Note 45: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - codebuild:BatchGetBuilds
                  # Note 46: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - codebuild:StartBuild
                Resource: !GetAtt CodeBuildProject.Arn
              # CodeStar Connection (GitHub)
              - Effect: Allow
                # Note 47: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Action:
                  - codestar-connections:UseConnection
                Resource: !Ref ConnectionArn
              # ECS deploy
              # Note 48: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ecs:DescribeServices
                  # Note 49: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - ecs:DescribeTaskDefinition
                  - ecs:DescribeTasks
                  - ecs:ListTasks
                  # Note 50: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  - ecs:RegisterTaskDefinition
                  - ecs:UpdateService
                Resource: "*"
              # Note 51: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - iam:PassRole
                # Note 52: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  StringEqualsIfExists:
                    # Note 53: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                    iam:PassedToService:
                      - ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com

  # ============================================================
  # CodeBuild Project — builds the Docker image
  # ============================================================
  CodeBuildProject:
    # Note 54: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
    Type: AWS::CodeBuild::Project
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${ProjectName}-build"
      # Note 55: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      Description: !Sub "Build Docker image for ${ProjectName}"
      ServiceRole: !GetAtt CodeBuildRole.Arn
      TimeoutInMinutes: 15
      # Note 56: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      Artifacts:
        Type: CODEPIPELINE
      Environment:
        # Note 57: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
        Type: LINUX_CONTAINER
        ComputeType: !Ref BuildComputeType
        Image: aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:5.0
        # Note 58: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        PrivilegedMode: true      # Required for Docker builds
        EnvironmentVariables:
          - Name: AWS_ACCOUNT_ID
            # Note 59: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            Value: !Ref AWS::AccountId
          - Name: AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
            Value: !Ref AWS::Region
          # Note 60: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          - Name: ECR_REPOSITORY
            Value: !Ref EcrRepositoryName
          - Name: CONTAINER_NAME
            # Note 61: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            Value: !Ref ContainerName
      Source:
        Type: CODEPIPELINE
        # Note 62: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        BuildSpec: |
          version: 0.2
          phases:
            # Note 63: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            pre_build:
              commands:
                - echo "Logging in to ECR..."
                # Note 64: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                - aws ecr get-login-password --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com
                - IMAGE_URI=$AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com/$ECR_REPOSITORY
                - IMAGE_TAG=$CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION
            # Note 65: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            build:
              commands:
                - echo "Building Docker image..."
                # Note 66: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                - docker build -t $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG .
                - docker tag $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG $IMAGE_URI:latest
            post_build:
              # Note 67: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              commands:
                - echo "Pushing Docker image..."
                - docker push $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG
                # Note 68: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                - docker push $IMAGE_URI:latest
                - echo "Writing image definitions for ECS deploy..."
                - printf '[{"name":"%s","imageUri":"%s"}]' "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG" > imagedefinitions.json
          # Note 69: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          artifacts:
            files:
              - imagedefinitions.json
      # Note 70: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      Tags:
        - Key: Project
          Value: !Ref ProjectName
        # Note 71: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: cloudformation

  # ============================================================
  # CodePipeline — Source → Build → Deploy
  # ============================================================
  Pipeline:
    # Note 72: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
    Type: AWS::CodePipeline::Pipeline
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${ProjectName}-pipeline"
      # Note 73: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      RoleArn: !GetAtt CodePipelineRole.Arn
      ArtifactStore:
        Type: S3
        # Note 74: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        Location: !Ref ArtifactBucket
      Stages:
        # --- Source Stage ---
        - Name: Source
          # Note 75: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          Actions:
            - Name: GitHubSource
              ActionTypeId:
                # Note 76: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Category: Source
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: CodeStarSourceConnection
                # Note 77: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Version: "1"
              Configuration:
                ConnectionArn: !Ref ConnectionArn
                # Note 78: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                FullRepositoryId: !Ref RepositoryId
                BranchName: !Ref BranchName
                DetectChanges: true
                # Note 79: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                OutputArtifactFormat: CODE_ZIP
              OutputArtifacts:
                - Name: SourceArtifact

        # --- Build Stage ---
        # Note 80: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        - Name: Build
          Actions:
            - Name: DockerBuild
              # Note 81: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Build
                Owner: AWS
                # Note 82: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Provider: CodeBuild
                Version: "1"
              Configuration:
                # Note 83: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                ProjectName: !Ref CodeBuildProject
              InputArtifacts:
                - Name: SourceArtifact
              # Note 84: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              OutputArtifacts:
                - Name: BuildArtifact

        # --- Manual Approval (optional — remove for auto-deploy) ---
        - Name: Approval
          # Note 85: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          Actions:
            - Name: ManualApproval
              ActionTypeId:
                # Note 86: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Category: Approval
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: Manual
                # Note 87: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Version: "1"
              Configuration:
                CustomData: !Sub "Approve deployment of ${ProjectName} to ECS?"
                # NotificationArn: arn:aws:sns:...   # <-- CHANGE THIS: SNS topic for approval emails

        # --- Deploy Stage ---
        # Note 88: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        - Name: Deploy
          Actions:
            - Name: DeployToECS
              # Note 89: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Deploy
                Owner: AWS
                # Note 90: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                Provider: ECS
                Version: "1"
              Configuration:
                # Note 91: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                ClusterName: !Ref EcsClusterName
                ServiceName: !Ref EcsServiceName
                FileName: imagedefinitions.json
              # Note 92: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
              InputArtifacts:
                - Name: BuildArtifact

      Tags:
        # Note 93: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        - Key: Project
          Value: !Ref ProjectName
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: cloudformation

# ============================================================
# OUTPUTS
# ============================================================
Outputs:
  PipelineUrl:
    Description: "CodePipeline console URL"
    Value: !Sub "https://${AWS::Region}.console.aws.amazon.com/codesuite/codepipeline/pipelines/${ProjectName}-pipeline/view"

  CodeBuildProjectName:
    Description: "CodeBuild project name"
    Value: !Ref CodeBuildProject

  ArtifactBucketName:
    Description: "S3 bucket for pipeline artifacts"
    Value: !Ref ArtifactBucket

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to AWS ECS
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying containerised apps to ECS Fargate/EC2
# PREREQUISITES: ECR repository, ECS cluster & service, task definition
# SECRETS NEEDED: AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: ECR repository, ECS cluster/service names, Dockerfile path
# RELATED FILES: docker/dotnet/Dockerfile.api
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Note 1: Resource identity and metadata drive automation, selectors, and operational traceability.
name: Deploy to AWS ECS

on:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  push:
    branches: [main]

# Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  ECR_REPOSITORY: my-app
  ECS_SERVICE: my-service
  # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  ECS_CLUSTER: my-cluster
  CONTAINER_NAME: my-app

# Note 6: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
jobs:
  deploy:
    # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    # Note 9: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN }}
          # Note 12: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to ECR
        # Note 13: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        id: login-ecr
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2

      # Note 14: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - name: Build and push to ECR
        id: build
        # Note 15: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
        run: |
          IMAGE_URI=${{ steps.login-ecr.outputs.registry }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ github.sha }}
          # Note 16: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          docker build -t $IMAGE_URI -f docker/dotnet/Dockerfile.api .
          docker push $IMAGE_URI
          # Note 17: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          echo "image=$IMAGE_URI" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Download task definition
        # Note 18: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
        run: aws ecs describe-task-definition --task-definition my-task --query taskDefinition > task-definition.json

      - name: Update ECS task definition
        id: task-def
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecs-render-task-definition@v1
        with:
          task-definition: task-definition.json
          container-name: ${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}
          image: ${{ steps.build.outputs.image }}

      - name: Deploy to ECS
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecs-deploy-task-definition@v1
        with:
          task-definition: ${{ steps.task-def.outputs.task-definition }}
          service: ${{ env.ECS_SERVICE }}
          cluster: ${{ env.ECS_CLUSTER }}
          wait-for-service-stability: true

# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to AWS EKS
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying to Amazon EKS via OIDC
# PREREQUISITES: EKS cluster, IAM role for OIDC
# SECRETS NEEDED: AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Cluster name, region, and Kustomize overlay path

# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Note 1: Resource identity and metadata drive automation, selectors, and operational traceability.
name: Deploy to AWS EKS

on:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

# Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  EKS_CLUSTER: my-eks-cluster

jobs:
  # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  deploy:
    if: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}
    # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    # Note 9: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - name: Configure AWS credentials (OIDC)
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN }}
          # Note 12: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Update kubeconfig
        # Note 13: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
        run: aws eks update-kubeconfig --name ${{ env.EKS_CLUSTER }} --region ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to ECR
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2

      - name: Deploy with Kustomize
        run: |
          kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
          kubectl rollout status deployment/app -n production

# GitLab CI — Deploy to AWS EKS

deploy-eks:
  stage: deploy
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.

    # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    - aws eks update-kubeconfig --name $EKS_CLUSTER_NAME --region $AWS_REGION

    url: https://app.example.com

# TEMPLATE: Azure Pipelines — Deploy to AKS
# WHEN TO USE: AKS deployment from Azure DevOps pipelines
# PREREQUISITES: AKS cluster, Azure service connection
# SECRETS NEEDED: AzureServiceConnection, AKS_RESOURCE_GROUP, AKS_CLUSTER_NAME
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Pipeline resource, environment name, namespace
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Azure Pipelines — Deploy to AKS
# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
trigger: none  # Triggered by CD pipeline, not directly

resources:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  pipelines:
    - pipeline: ci-build
      # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      source: CI-Build-Pipeline
      trigger:
        # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        branches:
          include: [main]

# Note 5: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
stages:
  - stage: Deploy
    # Note 6: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
    jobs:
      - deployment: DeployAKS
        # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        environment: production
        strategy:
          # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          runOnce:
            deploy:
              # Note 9: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
              steps:
                - task: KubernetesManifest@1
                  # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                  displayName: Deploy to AKS
                  inputs:
                    # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                    action: deploy
                    connectionType: azureResourceManager
                    azureSubscriptionConnection: $(AzureServiceConnection)
                    azureResourceGroup: $(AKS_RESOURCE_GROUP)
                    kubernetesCluster: $(AKS_CLUSTER_NAME)
                    namespace: production
                    manifests: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to Azure AKS
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying to Azure Kubernetes Service via OIDC
# PREREQUISITES: AKS cluster, Azure AD credentials
# SECRETS NEEDED: AZURE_CREDENTIALS
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Cluster name, resource group, namespace, image
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Note 1: Resource identity and metadata drive automation, selectors, and operational traceability.
name: Deploy to Azure AKS

on:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  workflow_run:
    workflows: [".NET Docker Build & Publish"]
    # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

# Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
env:
  CLUSTER_NAME: my-aks-cluster
  # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  RESOURCE_GROUP: my-resource-group
  NAMESPACE: production

# Note 6: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
jobs:
  deploy:
    # Note 7: Control flow should stay readable; predictable branches reduce defects and simplify troubleshooting.
    if: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    # Note 8: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - name: Azure Login
        uses: azure/login@v2
        # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        with:
          creds: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CREDENTIALS }}

      # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - name: Set AKS context
        uses: azure/aks-set-context@v3
        # Note 12: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        with:
          resource-group: ${{ env.RESOURCE_GROUP }}
          # Note 13: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          cluster-name: ${{ env.CLUSTER_NAME }}

      - name: Deploy with Helm
        uses: azure/k8s-deploy@v4
        with:
          namespace: ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}
          manifests: |
            cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
          images: |
            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:${{ github.sha }}

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to Azure App Service
# WHEN TO USE: Teams not yet on Kubernetes, using Azure PaaS
# PREREQUISITES: Azure Container Registry, App Service plan
# SECRETS NEEDED: AZURE_CREDENTIALS
# WHAT TO CHANGE: ACR name, App Service name, Dockerfile path
# RELATED FILES: docker/dotnet/Dockerfile.api
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Note 1: Resource identity and metadata drive automation, selectors, and operational traceability.
name: Deploy to Azure App Service

on:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  push:
    branches: [main]

# Note 3: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
jobs:
  deploy:
    # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Azure Login
        # Note 6: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
        uses: azure/login@v2
        with:
          # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          creds: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CREDENTIALS }}

      - name: Build and push Docker image
        # Note 8: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
        run: |
          az acr build \
            # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            --registry ${{ vars.ACR_NAME }} \
            --image my-app:${{ github.sha }} \
            # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
            --file docker/dotnet/Dockerfile.api \
            .

      # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - name: Deploy to App Service
        uses: azure/webapps-deploy@v3
        with:
          app-name: ${{ vars.APP_SERVICE_NAME }}
          images: "${{ vars.ACR_NAME }}.azurecr.io/my-app:${{ github.sha }}"

# TEMPLATE: GCP Cloud Build — Build & Deploy to GKE
# WHEN TO USE: Native GCP CI/CD pipeline for GKE deployments
# PREREQUISITES: Cloud Build API enabled, GKE cluster
# SECRETS NEEDED: None (uses Cloud Build service account)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Image name, cluster name, zone
# RELATED FILES: cd/targets/gcp-gke/github-actions-deploy.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Cloud Build — GKE deployment
# Note 1: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
steps:
  - name: "gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker"
    # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    args: ["build", "-t", "gcr.io/$PROJECT_ID/my-app:$COMMIT_SHA", "."]

  - name: "gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker"
    # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    args: ["push", "gcr.io/$PROJECT_ID/my-app:$COMMIT_SHA"]

  - name: "gcr.io/cloud-builders/kubectl"
    # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    args:
      - set
      # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - image
      - deployment/my-app
      # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - my-app=gcr.io/$PROJECT_ID/my-app:$COMMIT_SHA
    env:
      # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - "CLOUDSDK_COMPUTE_ZONE=us-central1-a"
      - "CLOUDSDK_CONTAINER_CLUSTER=my-gke-cluster"

# Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
images:
  - "gcr.io/$PROJECT_ID/my-app:$COMMIT_SHA"

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to GCP GKE
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying to Google Kubernetes Engine via Workload Identity
# PREREQUISITES: GKE cluster, Workload Identity Federation setup
# SECRETS NEEDED: WIF_PROVIDER, WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Project ID, cluster name, zone
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Note 1: Resource identity and metadata drive automation, selectors, and operational traceability.
name: Deploy to GCP GKE

on:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  push:
    branches: [main]

# Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
env:
  PROJECT_ID: my-gcp-project
  # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  GKE_CLUSTER: my-gke-cluster
  GKE_ZONE: us-central1-a

# Note 5: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
jobs:
  deploy:
    # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    # Note 8: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - name: Authenticate to Google Cloud
        uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
        # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        with:
          workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.WIF_PROVIDER }}
          # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          service_account: ${{ secrets.WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}

      - name: Get GKE credentials
        # Note 12: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
        uses: google-github-actions/get-gke-credentials@v2
        with:
          cluster_name: ${{ env.GKE_CLUSTER }}
          location: ${{ env.GKE_ZONE }}

      - name: Deploy
        run: |
          kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
          kubectl rollout status deployment/app

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: Azure Pipelines — Deploy to Red Hat OpenShift
# WHEN TO USE: OpenShift deployment from Azure DevOps pipelines
# PREREQUISITES: OpenShift cluster, service account token stored in Azure DevOps
# SECRETS NEEDED: OPENSHIFT_SERVER, OPENSHIFT_TOKEN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Pipeline resource, environment name, project/namespace, app name
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Azure Pipelines — Deploy to OpenShift
# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
trigger: none  # Triggered by CD pipeline, not directly

resources:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  pipelines:
    - pipeline: ci-build
      # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      source: CI-Build-Pipeline
      trigger:
        # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        branches:
          include: [main]

# Note 5: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
stages:
  - stage: Deploy
    # Note 6: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
    jobs:
      - deployment: DeployOpenShift
        # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        environment: production
        strategy:
          # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          runOnce:
            deploy:
              # Note 9: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
              steps:
                - script: |
                    # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                    curl -fsSL https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/stable/openshift-client-linux.tar.gz \
                      | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin oc
                    # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                    oc version --client
                  displayName: Install oc CLI

                # Note 12: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                - script: |
                    oc login $(OPENSHIFT_SERVER) --token=$(OPENSHIFT_TOKEN) --insecure-skip-tls-verify=false
                    # Note 13: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
                    oc project production
                  displayName: Log in to OpenShift

                - script: |
                    kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
                    kubectl rollout status deployment/$(APP_NAME) -n production
                  displayName: Deploy with Kustomize

                - script: |
                    oc get pods -n production -l app=$(APP_NAME)
                  displayName: Verify rollout

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to Red Hat OpenShift
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying to OpenShift via service account token
# PREREQUISITES: OpenShift cluster, service account with deploy permissions
# SECRETS NEEDED: OPENSHIFT_SERVER, OPENSHIFT_TOKEN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Server URL, project/namespace, image, app name
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Note 1: Resource identity and metadata drive automation, selectors, and operational traceability.
name: Deploy to OpenShift

on:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

# Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
env:
  OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE: production
  # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  APP_NAME: my-app
  IMAGE: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/production/my-app

# Note 6: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
jobs:
  deploy:
    # Note 7: Control flow should stay readable; predictable branches reduce defects and simplify troubleshooting.
    if: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      contents: read

    steps:
      # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to OpenShift
        # Note 11: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
        uses: redhat-actions/oc-login@v1
        with:
          # Note 12: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          openshift_server_url: ${{ secrets.OPENSHIFT_SERVER }}
          openshift_token: ${{ secrets.OPENSHIFT_TOKEN }}
          # Note 13: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          insecure_skip_tls_verify: false

      - name: Set project
        run: oc project ${{ env.OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE }}

      - name: Deploy with Kustomize
        run: |
          kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
          kubectl rollout status deployment/${{ env.APP_NAME }} -n ${{ env.OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE }}

      - name: Verify rollout
        run: oc get pods -n ${{ env.OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE }} -l app=${{ env.APP_NAME }}

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitLab CI — Deploy to Red Hat OpenShift
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying to OpenShift from GitLab CI/CD
# PREREQUISITES: OpenShift cluster, service account credentials in GitLab variables
# SECRETS NEEDED: OPENSHIFT_SERVER, OPENSHIFT_TOKEN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Project/namespace, app name, overlay path
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# GitLab CI — Deploy to Red Hat OpenShift
# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
deploy-openshift:
  stage: deploy
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  image: registry.redhat.io/openshift4/ose-cli:latest
  environment:
    # Note 3: Resource identity and metadata drive automation, selectors, and operational traceability.
    name: production
    url: https://app.example.com
  # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  script:
    - oc login $OPENSHIFT_SERVER --token=$OPENSHIFT_TOKEN --insecure-skip-tls-verify=false
    # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    - oc project $OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE
    - kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
    # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    - kubectl rollout status deployment/$APP_NAME -n $OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE
    - oc get pods -n $OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE -l app=$APP_NAME
  # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  only:
    - main

# TEMPLATE: Azure Pipelines — Deployment Gates & Approvals
# WHEN TO USE: Production deployments requiring manual approval or health checks
# PREREQUISITES: Azure DevOps environment with approval policies
# SECRETS NEEDED: None (configured in Azure DevOps UI)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Environment name and deployment steps
# RELATED FILES: ci/azure-pipelines/_strategies/variable-groups.yml
# MATURITY: Stable

# Azure Pipelines — Deployment Gates
# Gates pause deployment until conditions are met
# Note 1: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
stages:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - stage: Deploy
    # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    condition: and(succeeded(), eq(variables['Build.SourceBranch'], 'refs/heads/main'))
    # Note 4: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
    jobs:
      # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      - deployment: DeployProd
        # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        environment: production
        # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
        strategy:
          # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
          runOnce:
            preDeploy:
              steps:
                - script: echo "Pre-deploy checks"
            deploy:
              steps:
                - script: echo "Deploying..."

# In Azure DevOps environment settings, configure:
# - Approvals: require manual sign-off before deployment
# - Gates: query Azure Monitor, REST endpoint health checks, etc.
# - Branch policies: only deploy from protected branches

# Variable Groups — Linking to Azure Key Vault
# Reference this from your pipeline via group: name
# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
variables:
  # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - group: MyApp-Dev-Vars      # Define in Azure DevOps Library
  # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - group: MyApp-Dev-Secrets   # Linked to Azure Key Vault

# In Azure DevOps UI: Pipelines → Library → + Variable Group
# Toggle "Link secrets from an Azure key vault"
# Select your Key Vault and choose which secrets to sync
#
# Then reference in pipeline steps as: $(MY_SECRET)

# TEMPLATE: Azure Pipelines — Reusable Build Template
# WHEN TO USE: Standardise .NET build steps across pipelines
# PREREQUISITES: None
# SECRETS NEEDED: None
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Parameters when referencing this template
# RELATED FILES: ci/azure-pipelines/_templates/test-template.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Azure Pipelines — Build Template (extends)
# Usage: extends: { template: ci/azure-pipelines/_templates/build-template.yml }
# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
parameters:
  - name: buildConfiguration
    # Note 2: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
    type: string
    default: Release
  # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - name: dotnetVersion
    type: string
    # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    default: "8.0.x"

steps:
  # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - task: UseDotNet@2
    inputs:
      # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      version: ${{ parameters.dotnetVersion }}

  - task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
    # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    displayName: Restore
    inputs:
      # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      command: restore

  - task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
    # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    displayName: Build
    inputs:
      command: build
      arguments: --configuration ${{ parameters.buildConfiguration }} --no-restore

# Azure Pipelines — Docker Build Template
# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
parameters:
  - name: imageName
    # Note 2: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
    type: string
  - name: dockerfilePath
    # Note 3: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
    type: string
    default: Dockerfile
  # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - name: containerRegistry
    type: string

# Note 5: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
steps:
  - task: Docker@2
    # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    displayName: Build and push Docker image
    inputs:
      # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      command: buildAndPush
      repository: ${{ parameters.imageName }}
      # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      dockerfile: ${{ parameters.dockerfilePath }}
      containerRegistry: ${{ parameters.containerRegistry }}
      tags: |
        $(Build.SourceVersion)
        latest

# TEMPLATE: Azure Pipelines — Reusable Test Template
# WHEN TO USE: Standardise .NET test steps across pipelines
# PREREQUISITES: Test projects following naming convention
# SECRETS NEEDED: None
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Parameters when referencing this template
# RELATED FILES: ci/azure-pipelines/_templates/build-template.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Azure Pipelines — Test Template
# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
parameters:
  - name: testProjects
    # Note 2: This declaration defines a reusable unit, which supports composition and makes behavior easier to test.
    type: string
    default: "**/*Tests/*.csproj"

# Note 3: Pipeline structure separates concerns, helping teams test, deploy, and recover with smaller blast radius.
steps:
  - task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
    # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    displayName: Run tests
    inputs:
      # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      command: test
      projects: ${{ parameters.testProjects }}
      # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      arguments: --logger trx --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage"

  - task: PublishTestResults@2
    # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    condition: always()
    inputs:
      # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      testResultsFormat: VSTest
      testResultsFiles: "**/*.trx"

  # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
    inputs:
      summaryFileLocation: "**/coverage.cobertura.xml"

# TEMPLATE: Azure Pipelines — Python Build & Test
# WHEN TO USE: Python projects in Azure DevOps
# PREREQUISITES: requirements.txt and requirements-dev.txt
# SECRETS NEEDED: None
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: quality/python/pyproject.toml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Azure Pipelines — Python Build & Test
# Note 1: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
trigger:
  branches:
    # Note 2: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    include: [main, develop]

pool:
  # Note 3: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  vmImage: ubuntu-latest

strategy:
  # Note 4: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  matrix:
    Python311:
      # Note 5: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      python.version: "3.11"
    Python312:
      # Note 6: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      python.version: "3.12"

steps:
  # Note 7: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - task: UsePythonVersion@0
    inputs:
      # Note 8: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      versionSpec: $(python.version)

  - script: |
      # Note 9: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      pip install --upgrade pip
      pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-dev.txt
    # Note 10: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    displayName: Install dependencies

  - script: ruff check . && black --check .
    # Note 11: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
    displayName: Lint & Format check

  - script: |
      # Note 12: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
      pytest --junitxml=test-results.xml --cov=. --cov-report=xml
    displayName: Test

  # Note 13: This line contributes to the system's declarative intent, helping future readers reason about behavior and change impact.
  - task: PublishTestResults@2
    condition: always()
    inputs:
      testResultsFiles: test-results.xml

  - task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
    inputs:
      summaryFileLocation: coverage.xml
